title: Posit Conf 2025 — Agenda Chat
text:
About this deck
These slides walk through a Shiny chat app built for the Posit Conf 2025 agenda.
Press u to open the live app alongside the slides.

Note: The app is hosted on Posit Connect Cloud. If it doesn’t embed, use ↗ in the panel header to open it in a new tab.

title: Posit Conf 2025 — Agenda Chat
text:
About Posit Conf 2025
Posit Conf (formerly rstudio::conf) is the flagship annual event for the R and Python open-source data science community.

Hosted by Posit PBC — makers of RStudio, Quarto, Shiny
Sessions span keynotes, workshops, and lightning talks across two days

The 2025 edition featured sessions on:

AI + data science tooling — LLMs, RAG, vector stores
Quarto — reproducible documents and presentations
Shiny — interactive web apps in R and Python
Community — diversity, open source, collaboration

title: Posit Conf 2025 — Agenda Chat
text:
The app
Attendees can explore the conference schedule through natural conversation instead of digging through a static agenda page.

“What sessions are about LLMs or AI?”
“Who is speaking about Quarto?”
“What workshops are available on day 1?”
“Give me a summary of the keynotes.”

The assistant retrieves relevant session chunks from a vector store and generates a grounded answer — it won’t hallucinate sessions.

title: Posit Conf 2025 — Agenda Chat
text:
How it was built
A three-stage RAG pipeline built entirely in R over a weekend:
Conference website
        ↓  (web scraping with rvest)
  Structured agenda data
        ↓  (ragnar: chunk + embed + store)
  Vector store index
        ↓  (ellmer: retrieval + LLM)
  Shiny chat UI  ←→  User
Deployed to Posit Connect Cloud.

title: Posit Conf 2025 — Agenda Chat
text:
Key packages



Package
Role




shinychat
Streaming chat UI component for Shiny


ellmer
Unified LLM client (OpenAI, Anthropic, …)


ragnar
RAG framework: chunking, embedding, retrieval


rvest
Web scraping for the agenda data





shinychat docs
ellmer docs
ragnar docs

title: quarto-liveurl — Core Features
text:
What is quarto-liveurl?

Attach live URLs to individual slides
Toggle an overlay panel with u or the link button (top-right). You can try it on the next slide! It’s disabled on this one since there are no URLs attached.
Panel sits beside the slide — it doesn’t cover it
Works in iframe embed mode or as a link launcher

title: quarto-liveurl — Core Features
text:
Single URL — header shorthand
Add data-liveurl="..." to any slide header to attach one URL.
Press u — the panel opens and loads the page in an iframe.

Note: some sites block iframe embedding. Use the ↗ button in the panel header to open in a new tab as a fallback.

title: quarto-liveurl — Core Features
text:
Multiple URLs — block syntax


Quarto Presentations
Reveal.js Docs
Quarto Extensions


Three URLs → launcher list in auto mode.
Click a label to load it in the iframe. Use ← in the panel header to return to this list.

title: quarto-liveurl — Core Features
text:
Both slide and global URLs


Quarto Gallery


When a slide has URLs and liveurl.global is set, the launcher shows two sections: “This slide” and “Presentation”.
(No global URL is set in this file — see example-sticky.qmd for that.)

title: quarto-liveurl — Core Features
text:
No URLs on this slide
This slide has no data-liveurl attribute and no .liveurl block.
The link button (top-right) is dimmed and pressing u does nothing. Navigate to another slide to re-enable it.

title: quarto-liveurl — Core Features
text:
Persistent memory


Quarto Presentations
Reveal.js Docs
Quarto Extensions


rememberLastUrl: true (the default) stores the last URL you clicked, keyed to the presentation file path.
Try it: click one of the links above, close the panel, then reopen it — the same URL loads automatically.

title: quarto-liveurl — Sticky & Global URL
text:
Sticky + Global URL mode
This presentation has two special settings:

sticky: true — the panel content is preserved across slide transitions
liveurl.global — a URL available on every slide

Try it: Press u to open the panel, scroll down in the page, then navigate to the next slide. The page stays exactly where you left it.

title: quarto-liveurl — Sticky & Global URL
section: Slide 2 — panel still showing your page
text:
Slide 2 — panel still showing your page
The Quarto Guide is still loaded in the panel at the same scroll position.
Navigate freely — the page won’t reload until you close and reopen the panel.


Presentations Overview


This slide also has its own URL. The panel won’t switch to it automatically while sticky: true. Close and reopen the panel to load the new URL list.

title: quarto-liveurl — Sticky & Global URL
text:
Slide 3 — no slide-specific URLs
No data-liveurl or .liveurl block here.
The global URL is still accessible — the link button stays fully active on every slide because liveurl.global is set.
With sticky: true, the panel continues showing whatever was already loaded.

title: quarto-liveurl — Sticky & Global URL
text:
Slide 4 — when to use sticky
Good use cases for sticky: true:

LLM chat sessions — keep context as you walk through slides
Live dashboards — don’t interrupt auto-refresh or streaming data
Interactive apps — preserve user state (form inputs, selections)
Reference docs — let the audience read at their own pace while you advance

title: quarto-liveurl — Sticky & Global URL
text:
Slide 5 — resetting the panel


Quarto Extensions
Quarto Gallery


Try the reset flow here:

Press u to close the panel
Go back to slide 1 and press u to open (loads the Quarto Guide)
Navigate back to this slide, close the panel with u
Press u again — the panel now shows this slide’s URLs above

The panel always re-renders from the current slide on a fresh open.

title: quarto-liveurl
text: A Quarto Reveal.js extension for attaching and viewing live URLs during presentations.

title: quarto-liveurl
text:
Examples

Core Features — single URL, multiple URLs, launcher, no-URL slides
Sticky & Global URL — persistent panel across slide transitions
Paper Summary — “Attention Is All You Need” with the full paper open in the panel
Agenda Chat — Posit Conf 2025 RAG chat app as a persistent slide companion

title: Attention Is All You Need
text:
About this deck
These slides summarise Attention Is All You Need (Vaswani et al., NeurIPS 2017) — the paper that introduced the Transformer.

Press u to open the paper in the side panel
The panel auto-switches to the relevant section as you advance
Close and reopen on the same slide to restore your scroll position

title: Attention Is All You Need
text:
The Problem
Before Transformers, sequence modelling relied on RNNs and LSTMs.
Key limitations:

\(h_t\) depends on \(h_{t-1}\) → inherently sequential, hard to parallelise
Long sequences → vanishing gradients, early context is forgotten


“The dominant sequence transduction models are based on complex recurrent or convolutional neural networks.” — §1

Transformers replace recurrence with attention over the full sequence.

title: Attention Is All You Need
text:
Queries, Keys & Values
Each token produces three learned vectors:



Vector
Question it answers




Q (Query)
What am I looking for?


K (Key)
What do I offer others?


V (Value)
What do I contribute if selected?



Attention computes a weighted sum of Values, where the weights come from matching each Query against all Keys.

title: Attention Is All You Need
text:
Scaled Dot-Product Attention
\[\text{Attention}(Q, K, V) = \text{softmax}\!\left(\frac{QK^\top}{\sqrt{d_k}}\right)V\]

\(QK^\top\) — similarity between every query and every key
\(/ \sqrt{d_k}\) — scaling to prevent vanishing gradients from large dot products
\(\text{softmax}\) — normalise to a probability distribution over positions
\(\times V\) — weighted sum of value vectors

All positions are computed in parallel — no sequential dependency.

title: Attention Is All You Need
text:
Multi-Head Attention
Running attention once captures one type of relationship.
Multi-head attention runs it \(h\) times with different projections:
\[\text{MultiHead}(Q,K,V) = \text{Concat}(\text{head}_1,\ldots,\text{head}_h)\,W^O\]
\[\text{head}_i = \text{Attention}(QW^Q_i,\; KW^K_i,\; VW^V_i)\]
The paper uses \(h = 8\) heads, \(d_k = d_v = 64\).
Each head specialises in a different relationship — syntax, coreference, long-range dependencies — simultaneously.

title: Attention Is All You Need
text:
The Transformer Architecture
An encoder–decoder design with 6 layers each.
Every layer wraps each sub-layer with a residual connection + layer norm:
\[\text{Output} = \text{LayerNorm}(x + \text{Sublayer}(x))\]


Figure 1 — Architecture Diagram

title: Attention Is All You Need
text:
Encoder
Maps input tokens to continuous representations.
Each of the 6 encoder layers applies:
token embeddings + positional encoding
         ↓
 Multi-Head Self-Attention → Add & Norm
         ↓
 Feed-Forward Network      → Add & Norm
Self-attention is bidirectional — every position sees the full sequence.

title: Attention Is All You Need
text:
Decoder
Generates the output sequence one token at a time.
Each of the 6 decoder layers applies:
output embeddings + positional encoding
         ↓
 Masked Self-Attention     → Add & Norm
         ↓
 Cross-Attention (encoder) → Add & Norm
         ↓
 Feed-Forward Network      → Add & Norm
Masking prevents position \(i\) from attending to positions \(> i\).
Cross-attention reads from the encoder’s final representations.

title: Attention Is All You Need
text:
Positional Encoding
Attention is permutation-invariant — no built-in notion of position.
Fixed sinusoidal encodings are added to the input embeddings:
\[PE_{(pos,\,2i)}   = \sin\!\left(\tfrac{pos}{10000^{2i/d}}\right)\] \[PE_{(pos,\,2i+1)} = \cos\!\left(\tfrac{pos}{10000^{2i/d}}\right)\]

Different frequencies encode different positional scales
The model can learn to attend by relative offset
Generalises to sequence lengths not seen during training

title: Attention Is All You Need
text:
Training

Optimiser: Adam with a warm-up learning rate schedule
Regularisation: dropout 0.1 + label smoothing \(\epsilon_{ls} = 0.1\)
Hardware: 8 × P100 GPUs

The base model trained in 12 hours. Prior ensemble models took days.

title: Attention Is All You Need
text:
Results
WMT 2014 translation benchmarks:



Task
Transformer BLEU
Previous SOTA




English → German
28.4
26.4


English → French
41.0
40.5



New state of the art on both tasks — at a fraction of the training cost.

title: Attention Is All You Need
text:
Why It Mattered
The Transformer is now the backbone of nearly every large model.



Model
Year
Variant




BERT
2018
Encoder only


GPT series
2018–present
Decoder only


T5
2019
Encoder–decoder


ViT
2020
Encoder, image patches


LLaMA, Claude…
2023–present
Decoder only



Over 100,000 citations as of 2024.
